• The November 18 2025 Avoma call (32 minutes) with Kelley Benson shows explicit buyer engagement with the Zycus AI stack. The key-takeaway notes record that Kelley's team "currently uses Zycus for indirect CLM and Coupa for purchase requests while exploring AI-driven end-to-end S2P capabilities". Kelley asked specifically about Merlin, Anna and iRisk features.
• The agentic-platform discussion notes record Kelley Benson's team as interested in Zycus's AI-driven end-to-end S2P capabilities, with specific interest in agentic AI for conversational intake and autonomous negotiation, and Merlin with Microsoft Teams. AI importance is rated 'Very Critical'.
• The opportunity info reinforces an "openness to evaluating advanced procurement technologies" with multi-module potential. Being an existing Zycus CLM customer reduces friction. The score tips to 8 rather than 7 because of the "Very Critical" AI flag, buyer-side questions on autonomous negotiation specifics, and Merlin Intake, ANA and Agentic all in the product mix.

competition.narrative: Status Quo / Do Nothing is the primary threat. On the 18 November 2025 introductory call Kelley Benson stated current tools (Zycus CLM for Indirect contracts + Coupa for purchase requests) work 'pretty well' and her exploration is to 'broaden awareness', not solve a crisis. No compelling event, no quantified pain, no contract expiration, no regulatory driver, no cost crisis, no forcing function has been identified. Kelley's 7-month silence (18 Nov 2025 to 26 Jun 2026), repeated delays (21 Jan: 'push our discovery call to later in February', 20 Mar discovery call cancelled), and redirection to Imon Ahmed signal low priority or loss of interest. Without urgency, buyer will default to inertia. Coupa is the named competitor in Salesforce (Competitors__c: Coupa) and is the incumbent for P2P (purchase requests via supplier catalogs for office supplies and MRO, per Kelley on 18 Nov). Sentiment: neutral (Kelley said current tools work 'pretty well'). Coupa is not a direct threat unless CHEP runs a competitive RFP for full S2P replacement, which has not been discussed. The wedge: Zycus is the incumbent CLM provider, offers an organically built end-to-end S2P suite (vs Coupa P2P + Zycus CLM disconnected), and delivers AI-driven capabilities (Merlin Intake conversational procurement in Teams, Anna autonomous negotiation, iRisk continuous supplier risk monitoring) that Coupa does not natively provide. The activation: make the discovery call with Imon Ahmed and Kelley, quantify the manual-effort / tail-end-spend / compliance pain that the current fragmented stack creates, position Zycus as the unified S2P platform that eliminates the Coupa-CLM integration gap and unlocks AI-driven automation, and create a forcing function (budget cycle, Horizon event pull-in, executive sponsorship) to overcome status quo inertia.

identify_pain.narrative: On the 18 November 2025 introductory call Kelley Benson (Head of Indirect Source to Pay) articulated the operational pain directly: CHEP's Indirect procurement 'lacks a unified, intuitive mechanism for managing intake, routing, supplier interactions, and tail-end spend' and 'still depends heavily on manual effort, inconsistent routing, and fragmented workflows.' She confirmed current tools (Zycus CLM for Indirect contracts + Coupa for purchase requests via supplier catalogs) work 'pretty well' but the broader S2P process is disconnected, creating 'gaps in visibility, compliance, and efficiency.' Kelley stated her role tenure since July limits her depth and she is 'still mapping' several activities currently handled manually or through disconnected workflows. She wants to understand 'how AI and modern intake systems can streamline requests, improve compliance, and ease workload across sourcing, supplier management, and AP.' The pain is directional (manual effort, fragmented workflows, lack of visibility) but not quantified (no FTE hours, cycle time, error rate, or maverick spend % stated). The pain owner is Kelley Benson (Head of Indirect S2P), but she disclaimed decision authority and directed Marc to engage Imon Ahmed (Director of Global P2P) on 26 June 2026. Inferred pain: tail-end spend consolidation opportunities (Zycus pitch on 18 Nov mentioned '2-3% incremental tail-end savings'), supplier onboarding cycle time (Zycus pitch: 'accelerates supplier onboarding to around two days'), and policy compliance enforcement (Merlin Intake 'enforcing policies and routing requests'). Confirmed pain is process inefficiency and lack of unified S2P visibility; inferred pain is savings and cycle-time improvement, but buyer has not yet quantified either.

Zycus positioned Merlin Intake (conversational AI procurement in Teams), Anna (autonomous negotiation), iRisk (continuous supplier risk monitoring), organically built S2P suite (eliminating integration silos), 1,121+ APIs for seamless ERP connectivity, 2-3% incremental tail-end savings, and supplier onboarding acceleration to around two days.
